title: qualea selloi subsp. selloi
intro: Qualea selloi subsp. selloi, also known as the Quilea tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its bark, leaves, and roots containing compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Qualea selloi subsp. selloi include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Qualea selloi subsp. selloi are typically made by infusing the plant's parts in water or alcohol, and can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
